questions.question: The CEO decided to ________ the merger until the financial audit was fully completed to avoid any potential legal liabilities.
questions.explanation: defer 意為「推遲」。根據句意，執行長決定推遲合併案，直到財務審計完成。

questions.question: It is imperative that the marketing director ________ the proposal before the board meeting tomorrow morning.
questions.explanation: 在形容詞 imperative (必要的) 引導的 that 子句中，需使用虛擬語氣，動詞應為原形 review。

questions.question: The ________ of the new enterprise resource planning software has led to a significant increase in productivity across all departments.
questions.explanation: 此處需要名詞作為句子的主詞。implementation 意為「實施、執行」。

questions.question: ________ the complexity of the project, the project manager requested an extension of two weeks to ensure high-quality results.
questions.explanation: Given 作為介系詞，意為「考慮到」或「鑒於」，常用於句首說明理由。

questions.question: The company's annual report shows that profits have been ________ expectations for the third consecutive quarter.
questions.explanation: in excess of 意為「超過」。句意指利潤已連續三季超過預期。

questions.question: Mr. Henderson is the consultant ________ expertise helped the firm navigate the complex international trade regulations during the acquisition.
questions.explanation: whose 為關係代名詞所有格，用來修飾其後的名詞 expertise (專業知識)。

questions.question: Employees are encouraged to seek ________ from their supervisors regarding their performance goals during the annual review.
questions.explanation: clarification 意為「澄清、說明」。句意為鼓勵員工針對績效目標尋求主管的進一步說明。

questions.question: ________ the budget constraints, the research team managed to produce high-quality results within the specified deadline.
questions.explanation: Despite 是介系詞，後接名詞短語 budget constraints。Although 與 Even though 後需接子句。

questions.question: The legal team is ________ examining the contract terms to ensure there are no loopholes that could harm the company.
questions.explanation: meticulously 意為「細心地、嚴謹地」。法律團隊正在嚴謹地審查合約條款。

questions.question: Had the technical issues been reported sooner, the system upgrade ________ completed on schedule last month.
questions.explanation: 這是與過去事實相反的假設語氣，if 被省略而產生倒裝 (Had...)，主句應使用 would have + p.p.。

questions.question: Management is ______ to announce that the annual dividends will be increased by 15 percent this year due to record-breaking profits.
questions.explanation: be pleased to do something 為固定用法，表示「很榮幸、很高興做某事」，主詞通常為人或組織。

questions.question: Despite the _______ economic climate, the tech startup managed to secure another round of funding from its primary investors.
questions.explanation: adverse 為形容詞，意為「不利的、負面的」，在此修飾後方的名詞 economic climate（經濟氣候）。

questions.question: The CEO suggested that the marketing team _______ the promotional strategy before the next quarterly review to improve brand visibility.
questions.explanation: 在 suggest, recommend, insist 等動詞後的 that 子句中，動詞需用原形（省略了 should），此為擬議語氣 (Mandative Subjunctive)。

questions.question: The legal department has requested that all ______ information regarding the upcoming merger be stored exclusively on the encrypted server.
questions.explanation: confidential 為形容詞，意為「機密的」。在此修飾名詞 information。

questions.question: Mr. Henderson was ______ for the success of the merger, as his negotiation skills were instrumental in closing the deal.
questions.explanation: be credited for/with something 意為「歸功於...」或「因某事而受到讚揚」。

questions.question: The newly implemented software is ______ more efficient than the previous version, reducing processing time by nearly 40 percent.
questions.explanation: substantially 意為「大幅地、顯著地」，常用於修飾比較級 (more efficient)，形容提升程度巨大。

questions.question: Employees are strongly encouraged to _______ themselves of the professional development courses offered by the company during the summer break.
questions.explanation: avail oneself of something 是固定片語，意指「利用（機會、資源、優勢等）」。

questions.question: Had the board members known the full extent of the liabilities, they ______ the acquisition proposal during the last meeting.
questions.explanation: 此為與過去事實相反的假設語氣。從句 Had the board members known 為 If the board members had known 的倒裝，主句應使用 would have + p.p.。

questions.question: The project manager is looking for a candidate who possesses not only technical expertise ______ strong leadership qualities to guide the team.
questions.explanation: 相關連結詞 not only A but also B，意為「不僅 A 而且 B」。

questions.question: To ensure a seamless ______ to the new payroll system, all staff members must attend the mandatory training session on Friday afternoon.
questions.explanation: transition 意為「轉變、過渡」。seamless transition 指的是不同系統或階段之間的「無縫銜接」。

questions.question: The sales team must submit its quarterly forecast ________ Friday afternoon.
questions.explanation: 'By Friday afternoon' means no later than that time.
questions.questionZh: 業務團隊必須在週五下午以前提交季度預測。
questions.trapExplanationZh: 注意詞性、搭配與句意線索，其他選項不是詞性不合，就是語意方向錯誤。
optionReviewZh: by: 正確符合句意與文法結構。
until: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
during: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
within: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。

questions.question: Because the shipment was delayed, the warehouse manager arranged a ________ delivery for several key clients.
questions.explanation: A noun is needed to modify 'delivery'; 'priority delivery' is a common collocation.
questions.questionZh: 因為貨運延誤，倉庫經理為幾位重要客戶安排優先配送。
questions.trapExplanationZh: 注意詞性、搭配與句意線索，其他選項不是詞性不合，就是語意方向錯誤。
optionReviewZh: priority: 正確符合句意與文法結構。
prioritize: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
prioritized: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
prioritizing: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。

questions.question: Ms. Chen asked the technician to inspect the printers ________ the training session began.
questions.explanation: 'Before' correctly connects the two time clauses.
questions.questionZh: 陳女士要求技術人員在訓練課開始前檢查印表機。
questions.trapExplanationZh: 注意詞性、搭配與句意線索，其他選項不是詞性不合，就是語意方向錯誤。
optionReviewZh: before: 正確符合句意與文法結構。
despite: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
unless: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
whether: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。

questions.question: The conference room is ________ equipped with video screens, microphones, and adjustable lighting.
questions.explanation: An adverb is needed to modify 'equipped'.
questions.questionZh: 這間會議室完整配備螢幕、麥克風和可調式照明。
questions.trapExplanationZh: 注意詞性、搭配與句意線索，其他選項不是詞性不合，就是語意方向錯誤。
optionReviewZh: fully: 正確符合句意與文法結構。
full: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
fuller: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
fullness: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。

questions.question: All employees are expected to read the updated travel policy ________ making any reservations.
questions.explanation: 'Prior to' means before and fits the timing of the action.
questions.questionZh: 所有員工在訂位前都應閱讀更新後的差旅政策。
questions.trapExplanationZh: 注意詞性、搭配與句意線索，其他選項不是詞性不合，就是語意方向錯誤。
optionReviewZh: prior to: 正確符合句意與文法結構。
instead of: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
except for: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
because of: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。

questions.question: The new inventory system allows staff to locate products more ________ than before.
questions.explanation: The comparative phrase modifies the verb 'locate', so an adverb is required.
questions.questionZh: 新的庫存系統讓員工能比以前更有效率地定位商品。
questions.trapExplanationZh: 注意詞性、搭配與句意線索，其他選項不是詞性不合，就是語意方向錯誤。
optionReviewZh: efficiently: 正確符合句意與文法結構。
efficient: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
efficiency: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
efficiencies: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。

questions.question: The committee will review the application only after all required documents ________ submitted.
questions.explanation: The present perfect passive is needed after 'after' to describe completed submission.
questions.questionZh: 委員會只會在所有必要文件都已提交後審查申請。
questions.trapExplanationZh: 注意詞性、搭配與句意線索，其他選項不是詞性不合，就是語意方向錯誤。
optionReviewZh: have been: 正確符合句意與文法結構。
having been: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
to be: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
being: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。

questions.question: Please contact the front desk if you need ________ about nearby restaurants.
questions.explanation: A plural noun is needed after 'need'.
questions.questionZh: 如果你需要附近餐廳的推薦，請聯絡櫃台。
questions.trapExplanationZh: 注意詞性、搭配與句意線索，其他選項不是詞性不合，就是語意方向錯誤。
optionReviewZh: recommendations: 正確符合句意與文法結構。
recommends: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
recommended: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
recommending: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。

questions.question: The director praised the team for completing the project ________ the limited budget.
questions.explanation: 'Within the budget' means not exceeding the budget.
questions.questionZh: 主管稱讚團隊在有限預算內完成專案。
questions.trapExplanationZh: 注意詞性、搭配與句意線索，其他選項不是詞性不合，就是語意方向錯誤。
optionReviewZh: within: 正確符合句意與文法結構。
along: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
toward: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
among: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。

questions.question: The software update will be installed automatically, ________ users have disabled that setting.
questions.explanation: 'Unless' introduces the exception to the automatic installation.
questions.questionZh: 除非使用者停用該設定，否則軟體更新會自動安裝。
questions.trapExplanationZh: 注意詞性、搭配與句意線索，其他選項不是詞性不合，就是語意方向錯誤。
optionReviewZh: unless: 正確符合句意與文法結構。
while: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
although: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
so that: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。

questions.question: Our customer service representatives respond to most inquiries ________ twenty-four hours.
questions.explanation: 'Within twenty-four hours' describes a time limit.
questions.questionZh: 客服代表通常會在二十四小時內回覆大多數詢問。
questions.trapExplanationZh: 注意詞性、搭配與句意線索，其他選項不是詞性不合，就是語意方向錯誤。
optionReviewZh: within: 正確符合句意與文法結構。
between: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
over: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
onto: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。

questions.question: The board approved the proposal because it was both practical and ________.
questions.explanation: 'Cost-effective' is the correct adjective meaning economical.
questions.questionZh: 董事會批准該提案，因為它實用且符合成本效益。
questions.trapExplanationZh: 注意詞性、搭配與句意線索，其他選項不是詞性不合，就是語意方向錯誤。
optionReviewZh: cost-effective: 正確符合句意與文法結構。
costing-effect: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
cost-effect: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
costs-effective: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。

questions.question: The hotel offers a complimentary shuttle service for guests ________ flights arrive after 9 P.M.
questions.explanation: 'Whose' shows possession: guests whose flights arrive.
questions.questionZh: 飯店為班機在晚上九點後抵達的旅客提供免費接駁。
questions.trapExplanationZh: 注意詞性、搭配與句意線索，其他選項不是詞性不合，就是語意方向錯誤。
optionReviewZh: whose: 正確符合句意與文法結構。
which: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
whom: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
what: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。

questions.question: A final decision regarding the contract will be announced ________ the legal review is complete.
questions.explanation: 'Once' means as soon as and fits the timing.
questions.questionZh: 合約的最終決定會在法律審查完成後公布。
questions.trapExplanationZh: 注意詞性、搭配與句意線索，其他選項不是詞性不合，就是語意方向錯誤。
optionReviewZh: once: 正確符合句意與文法結構。
despite: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
whereas: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
provided: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。

questions.question: To reduce energy costs, lights in unused offices should be turned off ________.
questions.explanation: An adverb is needed to modify the passive verb phrase.
questions.questionZh: 為了降低能源成本，未使用辦公室的燈應立即關閉。
questions.trapExplanationZh: 注意詞性、搭配與句意線索，其他選項不是詞性不合，就是語意方向錯誤。
optionReviewZh: promptly: 正確符合句意與文法結構。
prompt: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
promptness: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
prompted: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。

questions.question: The candidate was selected because of her ________ experience in international logistics.
questions.explanation: An adjective is needed before 'experience'.
questions.questionZh: 這位候選人因具有豐富的國際物流經驗而被選上。
questions.trapExplanationZh: 注意詞性、搭配與句意線索，其他選項不是詞性不合，就是語意方向錯誤。
optionReviewZh: extensive: 正確符合句意與文法結構。
extension: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
extensively: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
extend: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。

questions.question: The repair work has been postponed ________ severe weather conditions in the area.
questions.explanation: 'Due to' introduces the reason for the postponement.
questions.questionZh: 維修工作因當地天候惡劣而延期。
questions.trapExplanationZh: 注意詞性、搭配與句意線索，其他選項不是詞性不合，就是語意方向錯誤。
optionReviewZh: due to: 正確符合句意與文法結構。
instead: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
whether: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
therefore: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。

questions.question: Employees who park in the visitor area may be asked to move ________ vehicles.
questions.explanation: A possessive adjective is needed before 'vehicles'.
questions.questionZh: 停在訪客區的員工可能會被要求移動自己的車輛。
questions.trapExplanationZh: 注意詞性、搭配與句意線索，其他選項不是詞性不合，就是語意方向錯誤。
optionReviewZh: their: 正確符合句意與文法結構。
theirs: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
them: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
themselves: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。

questions.question: The accounting department requested that each receipt ________ attached to the expense report.
questions.explanation: After 'request that', the base-form subjunctive 'be' is used.
questions.questionZh: 會計部門要求每張收據都附在費用報告中。
questions.trapExplanationZh: 注意詞性、搭配與句意線索，其他選項不是詞性不合，就是語意方向錯誤。
optionReviewZh: be: 正確符合句意與文法結構。
is: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
was: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
being: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。

questions.question: The training materials are available online for employees who were ________ to attend the workshop.
questions.explanation: 'Unable to attend' is the correct adjective phrase.
questions.questionZh: 無法參加工作坊的員工可以在線上取得訓練資料。
questions.trapExplanationZh: 注意詞性、搭配與句意線索，其他選項不是詞性不合，就是語意方向錯誤。
optionReviewZh: unable: 正確符合句意與文法結構。
unablely: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
unability: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
disable: 不符合空格前後的文法或語意。
